item.title: Is Iran Winning?
item.description: The Iran expert Suzanne Maloney explains why Iran believes it has the upper hand.

item.title: Trump Has Lost Control of the Iran War
item.description: The pain inflicted on Iran has not forced it into submission. It is not clear what will.

item.title: Is It Legal to Bully the Supreme Court?
item.description: It may be a new world, but “it’s the same Constitution.”

item.title: How Giorgia Meloni Fell to Earth
item.description: For more than three years, Giorgia Meloni’s leadership of Italy has seemed unshakable. Now she suddenly seems vulnerable.

item.title: The Epstein Emails Show #MeToo Never Stood a Chance
item.description: The files reveal the mechanics of doubt that the #MeToo movement had to contend with.

item.title: What Trump Is Doing to the English Language
item.description: There is a morality to verbs, especially in political speech.

item.title: Every President Tries It. It Never Works.
item.description: What’s really behind all these attempts to revive American industry?

item.title: The Rise and Fall of Pam Bondi
item.description: Readers react to President Trump’s firing of the attorney general. Also: A.I. and therapy.

item.title: All the Worst People Seem to Want to Be ‘High Agency’
item.description: The term “high agency” is an ethos for a gambler’s time.

item.title: Trump Has No Idea How to Clean Up His Own Mess
item.description: His speech told us very little, at least explicitly, but revealed quite a lot.

item.title: The One Thing Trump Wanted That Pam Bondi Failed to Deliver
item.description: The core of Mr. Trump’s dissatisfaction with the attorney general was apparently her failure to serve his need for revenge against his enemies.

item.title: Gooning Toward Trump
item.description: The scholar Henry Farrell explains how efforts to capture Trump’s attention and approval through spectacle resemble the attention dynamics in Nazi Germany.

item.title: Why Does Gavin Newsom Sound Like a Right-Wing Troll?
item.description: Gavin Newsom seems to have concluded that progressive models of manliness are passé.

item.title: Why Iran Believes It Has the Upper Hand
item.description: The Iran expert Suzanne Maloney explains how Iran views the war, and why the United States is losing.

item.title: Consciousness Lives in Our Bodies
item.description: “The brain exists to keep the body alive, not the other way around,” argues the author Michael Pollan as he explores the connection between consciousness and the body in this episode of “The Ezra Klein Show.”

item.title: Did Jesus Rise From the Dead?
item.description: A case of mistaken identity, or a profound experience of grief? The religious scholar Bart Ehrman argues that the earliest accounts of Jesus’ Resurrection might be rooted in the way we process loss.

item.title: How Did Trump’s Iran Speech Fare?
item.description: Readers respond to President Trump’s speech on the war. Also: Asian Americans at risk; civic eduction for the 250th; a veteran’s view.

item.title: How Jesus Altered the World’s Conscience
item.description: It might sound obvious now, but helping your neighbor wasn’t always seen as a moral imperative. The New Testament scholar Bart Ehrman explains how Jesus’ teachings shape the way we all view our role in society today.

item.title: Did Jesus Rise From the Dead? A Debate.
item.description: A ‘Christian Atheist’ joins Ross Douthat.

item.title: Eloquence Has Grown Passé. Let’s Bring It Back Into Fashion.
item.description: Being authentic or relatable shouldn’t mean being inarticulate.

item.title: Stolid Midwestern Republicans Might Be the Real #Resistance
item.description: In the Indiana primaries, a crucial test of the MAGA loyalty machine.

item.title: Trump Isn’t Taking Cuba. He’s Starving It.
item.description: Starving Cubans does nothing to advance American interests.

item.title: On Iran, Trump Offers No Plan and No End in Sight
item.description: The president failed to provide any meaningful strategy on the two central issues that define the war on Iran: reopening the Strait of Hormuz and what to do about that country’s uranium.

item.title: Even if This Is the Right War, We’ve Got the Wrong President
item.description: Tastelessness and classlessness are the least of our concerns.

item.title: Trump’s Positive Thinking Won’t End This War
item.description: President Trump’s Wednesday night address about the war in Iran is evidence of how poorly his war of choice is going, the columnist Jamelle Bouie argues.

item.title: Trump Will Lose the Birthright Citizenship Case. But in a Way, He’s Already Won.
item.description: The case is open-and-shut on the merits. But its procedural course still leaves plenty of room for Trump administration overreach.

item.title: Did Jesus Rise From the Dead? A Debate.
item.description: A ‘Christian Atheist’ joins Ross Douthat.

item.title: What Do You Do When a Family Member Commits a Terrible Crime?
item.description: The columnist M. Gessen sits down with the writer Harriet Clark to talk through the complexity of maintaining relationships with family members who have done unthinkable things.

item.title: To Keep Child Abuse Off the Internet, He Has to Watch It
item.description: Removing child sexual abuse material from the internet exacts a heavy toll on the workers tasked with reviewing it.

item.title: 10 Reports in 10 Minutes
item.description: Removing child sexual abuse material from the internet exacts a heavy toll on the workers tasked with reviewing it.

item.title: This War Is Bad for America
item.description: We owe it to American service members and their families to be truthful about the risks involved and why we are at war.

item.title: Trump’s Fixation on This Phrase Reveals Something About the War in Iran
item.description: In Iran, the schedule is our most malleable illusion.

item.title: Remember the Oil Shocks of the ’70s? This Is Going to Be Worse. Much Worse.
item.description: Higher oil prices and limited supply will accelerate a turn toward cleaner technologies, especially in Asia and Europe.

item.title: A Supreme Court Ruling for Discredited Gay Conversion Therapy
item.description: Readers discuss the justices’ decision on gay conversion therapy. Also: An immigrant’s story; a list of Jews at Penn?

item.title: Padma Lakshmi: The Decision That Would Create a Permanent American Underclass
item.description: Trump wants to take away a fundamental American promise.

item.title: She Was a Famous Millennial Feminist. Her Polyamory Memoir Is Heartbreaking.
item.description: Almost every ideology can be wielded to make women feel that they’re failing.

item.title: The Greatest Threat to Our Elections Is No Longer Foreign
item.description: This is not how American democracy is supposed to work.

item.title: The Birthright Con
item.description: Trump wants to find out just how weak this Supreme Court is.

item.title: The ‘Constitutional Bedrock’ Trump Is Trying to Erase
item.description: Trump is on a mission to eliminate birthright citizenship, a right long thought to be guaranteed by the 14th Amendment of the Constitution. But how will the Supreme Court respond? The legal journalist Cristian Farias shares his insights.

item.title: Can Christianity Be Restored to the Center of American Life?
item.description: Why none of America’s faiths have taken mainline Protestantism’s place.

item.title: Trump Has a Way Out of the Iran War
item.description: Trump should set aside his 15-point peace plan and reduce it to these two points.

item.title: The More You Study Consciousness, the Weirder It Gets
item.description: Michael Pollan, a science writer, spent five years trying to understand how consciousness worked.

item.title: Michael Pollan’s Journey to the Borderlands of Consciousness
item.description: Pollan, a science writer, spent five years trying to understand how consciousness worked. The more he learned, the weirder things got.

item.title: Yes, This Is Your War, Too
item.description: Even opponents of the conflict should want to see Iran defanged and defeated.

item.title: The Democrats’ Looming 2032 Cliff
item.description: The 2030 census is very likely to hurt their electoral prospects.

item.title: I Wrote a Cesar Chavez Biography. This Is How His Secrets Stayed Buried.
item.description: Reckoning with the United Farm Workers founder means reckoning with his movement.

item.title: Can You Anesthetize a Plant?
item.description: If it’s possible to put plants to sleep using anesthesia, what does that say about whether they’re conscious? Can they feel pain? The author Michael Pollan explores this “spooky and interesting” discovery on “The Ezra Klein Show.”

item.title: Trump, Don’t Repeat Churchill’s Deadly Mistake
item.description: A 1936 convention establishing Turkish control over the Dardanelles could be an inspiration to resolving the energy bottleneck in the Persian Gulf.

item.title: An Ominous U.S. Threat Against Iran
item.description: Readers criticize President Trump over his bellicose statements on Iran. Also: Education for every child, citizen or not; the cost of war.

item.title: The People Trump Pardoned Are on a Crime Spree
item.description: The president and the Republican Party deserve to pay a political price for the pardons.
